name: rad-session
description: Three-phase Claude Code workflow lifecycle with cross-machine continuity. /init bootstraps a project once (deterministic stack detection via Python scripts, CLAUDE.md scaffold, gitignore handling for session files, rad-* plugin recommendations, plus a per-project plugin-bloat audit that proposes enabledPlugins disables). /startup orients each session (Phase 0 fetches origin and prompts to pull when behind before reading any handoff file; cross-machine handoff detection via hostname; cached resource discovery; stale-handoff auto-refresh from git log). /wrapup captures each session (recency-bounded conversation tagging, structured HANDOFF.md with 'what NOT to do' field, mechanical session-log derivation from HANDOFF, CLAUDE.md prune auto-skipped when unchanged, auto-commit + prompted push of session files). /add-resource registers tools any time. Three Python scripts (detect-stack.py, detect-resources.py, audit-plugin-bloat.py) make stack, MCP, and plugin-bloat detection deterministic. PreCompact hook prevents silent context loss. 3.7 fixes the v3.5 regression that pinned /startup and /wrapup to Haiku 4.5 — the pin caused 'context used up' errors whenever a 1M-context Opus session exceeded Haiku's 200K window before the skill was invoked. Skills now run in the session model; if you want Haiku speed on a short wrapup, run /model haiku explicitly before invoking the skill so the context-window trade-off is your choice.
